In [28]:
# cleaning 
# remove duplicate 
data = data.drop_duplicates()
# convert nulls to 0 
data['total_laid_off'] = data['total_laid_off'].fillna(0)
data['percentage_laid_off'] = data['percentage_laid_off'].fillna(0)
data['funds_raised_millions'] = data['funds_raised_millions'].fillna(0)
In [55]:
# dealing with nulls to 0 
data['total_laid_off'] = data['total_laid_off'].fillna(0)
data['percentage_laid_off'] = data['percentage_laid_off'].fillna(0)
data['funds_raised_millions'] = data['funds_raised_millions'].fillna(0)
# drop data of nulls
data =data.dropna(subset=['industry','date','stage'])
print(data.isnull().sum())
# convert date type 
date = data['date']=pd.to_datetime(data['date'])
date = data['year']=data['date'].dt.year
data['month'] = data['date'].dt.month_name()

In [33]:
# calculate yearly total lay off 
yearly_total_laid_off = data.groupby('year')['total_laid_off'].sum()
print("total_laied_off per year")
print (yearly_total_laid_off )

total_laied_off per year
year

2021     15823.0

In [44]:
#graph of total_laid_off per year
yearly_total_laid_off.plot(kind='line', marker='o', figsize=(10,5),color='black')
plt.title('Layoffs Trend Over Time')
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('Total Employees Laid Off')
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()
In [46]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7))
sns.scatterplot(x='funds_raised_millions', y='total_laid_off', hue='industry', data=data)
plt.title('The Funding Paradox: Total Funding vs Layoffs')
plt.xlabel('Funds Raised (Millions)')
plt.ylabel('Total Laid Off')
plt.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc='upper left') 
plt.show()
In [49]:
# industry distribution 
industry_laid_off = data.groupby('industry')['total_laid_off'].sum().sort_values(ascending=False)
plt.figure(figsize=(15, 8))
sns.barplot(x=industry_laid_off.values,y=industry_laid_off.index,hue=industry_laid_off.index,palette='magma')
plt.title('Total Layoffs by Industry')
plt.xlabel('Number of Employees Laid Off')
plt.ylabel('Industry')
plt.show()
In [59]:
# patterns of employee layoffs 
# piviot tables 
pivot_table = data.pivot_table(index='month', columns='year', values='total_laid_off', aggfunc='sum').fillna(0)
# arrangment of monthes 
months_order = ['January', 'February', 'March', 'April', 'May', 'June', 'July', 'August', 'September', 'October', 'November', 'December']
pivot_table = pivot_table.reindex(months_order)
#gragh
plt.figure(figsize=(15, 8))
sns.heatmap(pivot_table, annot=True, fmt=".0f", cmap="YlOrRd")
plt.title('Layoffs Heatmap: Months vs Years')
plt.show()
In [67]:
#total layoffs
total_layoffs = data['total_laid_off'].sum()
# top companies laid_off 
top_company = data.groupby('company')['total_laid_off'].sum().idxmax()
#Top company value 
top_company_value = data.groupby('company')['total_laid_off'].sum().max()
#worset industry laid off
worst_industry = data.groupby('industry')['total_laid_off'].sum().idxmax()
#worthst year 
worst_year = data.groupby('year')['total_laid_off'].sum().idxmax()
# prints 
print("-" * 30)
print("📌 LAYOFFS ANALYSIS REPORT")
print("-" * 30)
print(f"🔹 Total Employees Laid Off: {total_layoffs:,.0f}")
print(f"🔹 Most Affected Company: {top_company} ({top_company_value:,.0f} employees)")
print(f"🔹 Hardest Hit Industry: {worst_industry}")
print(f"🔹 Year with Maximum Layoffs: {worst_year}")
print("-" * 30)

# display top 5 companies by inpact 
print("\n🚀 Top 5 Companies by Impact:")
print(data.groupby('company')['total_laid_off'].sum().nlargest(5))

------------------------------
📌 LAYOFFS ANALYSIS REPORT

🔹 Most Affected Company: Amazon (18,150 employees)
🔹 Hardest Hit Industry: Consumer
🔹 Year with Maximum Layoffs: 2022

In [72]:
from wordcloud import WordCloud
company_dict = data.groupby('company')['total_laid_off'].sum().to_dict()
wordcloud = WordCloud(width=800, height=400, background_color='white', colormap='Dark2').generate_from_frequencies(company_dict)
plt.figure(figsize=(15, 10))
plt.imshow(wordcloud, interpolation='bilinear')
plt.axis("off")
plt.title('Layoffs Magnitude by Company (Size = Total Employees Lost)')
plt.show()
In [73]:
#save image 
wordcloud.to_file("layoffs_wordcloud.png")
print("Image saved! 📸")
